_README.purpose: Flux depth ControlNet workflow — uses a C4D render as a spatial reference to guide background generation. The depth map extracted from the C4D scene tells Flux where the platform, stage floor, and negative space are, so the generated background matches the scene composition. This is the 'digital twin' approach: the 3D stage geometry informs the AI image rather than generating a background blind.
required_setup: 2. Install ComfyUI-ControlNet-Aux custom nodes (for the depth preprocessor):
Open ComfyUI > Manager > Install Custom Nodes

node_map.7: CLIPTextEncode    — empty negative (Flux doesn't use negatives, but ControlNetApply needs the slot)
node_map.8: VAEDecode         — converts latent to final pixel image
node_map.9: SaveImage         — saves output PNG (filename_prefix injected by Python)
node_map.11: DualCLIPLoader    — loads clip_l + t5xxl for Flux
node_map.22: BasicGuider       — takes conditioning from ControlNetApply (node 33), not directly from CLIP
node_map.25: RandomNoise       — seed (injected by Python for unique results each run)
node_map.30: LoadImage         — the C4D reference render (filename injected by Python after upload)
node_map.32: AIO_Preprocessor  — extracts depth map from C4D render using DepthAnythingV2
node_map.33: ControlNetApplyAdvanced — applies depth guidance to the Flux conditioning

_README.how_it_works: The C4D render goes into LoadImage(30) → AIO_Preprocessor(32) extracts a depth map (grayscale image showing what is close vs far in the scene) → ControlNetApplyAdvanced(33) uses that depth map to steer the Flux generation so it respects the spatial composition of the stage. The output background will match the platform depth, perspective, and negative space layout of your Cinema 4D scene.
_README.strength_guide: The 'strength' value in node 33 controls how tightly the output follows the C4D depth reference. 0.5 = loose reference (AI has more freedom), 0.75 = strong guidance (closely matches stage layout), 1.0 = very constrained. 0.65 is a good starting point.

python_injection_fields.node_6_text: The scene description prompt (built from brief)
python_injection_fields.node_9_filename_prefix: Unique job prefix so Python can find the output
python_injection_fields.node_25_noise_seed: Random seed (Python injects a UUID-based seed)
python_injection_fields.node_30_image: Filename of uploaded C4D render (Python uploads it first)
python_injection_fields.node_33_strength: How tightly to follow the depth reference (0.5–0.85)

inputs.text: intimate jazz club interior, cool blue ambient stage lighting, dark atmospheric background, smooth polished floor reflection, subtle bokeh depth of field, cinematic photography, premium feel, no saxophone, no instruments

_meta.title: Negative Prompt (empty — Flux does not use negative prompts)
Guider — conditioning now comes from ControlNet (node 33), not directly from CLIP
C4D Reference Render — filename injected by Python after upload
Flux Depth ControlNet — download from XLabs-AI on HuggingFace
Depth Preprocessor — requires ComfyUI-ControlNet-Aux custom nodes
Apply Depth ControlNet — strength 0.65 balances scene fidelity vs AI freedom
